sections.heading: 0. Frame
sections.body: Four strands from the objective: (1) reconcile docs with built reality; (2) user-facing install instructions; (3) the repo as an Obsidian vault (one cross-linked graph rooted at README); (4) an adversarial, humble README that leads with why NOT to use lume and evidences or flags every claim. This iteration produces findings + forks only - no doc rewrites, no engine code. Built reality to reconcile against: lume is v0.1.0, a packaged Claude Code plugin (plugin/ root, engine at plugin/src/lume, run via plugin/bin/lume), 313 passing tests, 8 closed workstreams, dogfooded (lume built lume). The docs/ set predates almost all of that.

sections.heading: 1. Doc inventory + classification
sections.body:
Prose docs (excluding .pytest_cache/README.md, which is gitignored tool junk):
- README.md (repo root, dev-oriented, written 0008): UPDATE - this becomes the adversarial, humble, vault-root README (strand 4) and the install front door (strand 2). Currently accurate but makes unevidenced claims and leads with benefits, not disqualifiers.
- plugin/README.md (adopter-facing front door, 0008): UPDATE - keep install-focused but align tone/claims with the honesty rule and cross-link; resolve duplication-with-root (fork F2/F4).
- plugin/src/lume/README.md (engine internals, 0008): KEEP (light touch) - current after the plugin/ move; add cross-links into the graph.
- plugin/skills/lume/SKILL.md (the guiding skill): KEEP - it is product, verified accurate against TRANSITIONS in 0008; not a free-prose doc, but link to/from it.
- docs/vision.md: PRESERVE-AS-HISTORICAL - strong intent statement but written as pre-build aspiration; success criteria it sets are now met. Banner it as a design-record + reconcile the 'success' section.
- docs/scope.md: PRESERVE-AS-HISTORICAL - 'v1 spine / in scope for v1 / first slice / priority sequence' is a plan that mostly happened; preserve with a status banner and a pointer to what shipped.
- docs/constraints.md: UPDATE-or-BANNER - the non-negotiables still hold and are load-bearing (keep, current), but the dated decision log is historical; split 'still-true constraints' from 'decision log (historical)'.
- docs/questions.md: UPDATE - 'load-bearing assumptions to validate' and risks are partly resolved by 8 workstreams; reconcile each assumption to validated / still-open with evidence.
- ADOPTERS.md (root): CREATE (new, strand 5) - a registry where adopters self-register (name/handle + project link). Starts with lume itself as the sole adopter; near-empty BY DESIGN, which is itself evidence for the 'zero external users' disqualifier. Structure as a consistent, parseable entry shape so lume can later read it to find adopters and gather usage insights. Part of the vault graph (linked from README).

sections.heading: 2. Reconciliation list (claim vs reality)
sections.body:
Concrete disagreements execution must fix or annotate:
- scope.md 'v1 spine = manage lume's own construction' and the priority sequence (1 linear loop ... 6 templates ... 7 multi-operator): items 1-2 done, 3-4 (nesting, flat queue) still deferred, packaging (not in the original sequence) done. Reconcile.
- vision.md Success: 'leading signal: lume used to build lume' = MET (8 workstreams); 'lagging outcome: good enough to point at another repo' = MET (0008 packaging) - but only mechanically proven, not lived. State both, with the caveat.
- questions.md assumptions: #1 honest self-review and #4 ceremony-pays-for-itself remain UNMEASURED (no measurement workstream); #2 crisp DoD and #3 2-min re-orient are exercised but not metricised. Mark each validated/partial/open with evidence.
- LICENSE: plugin.json declares "license": "MIT" but there is NO LICENSE file in the repo. Either add MIT LICENSE or correct the manifest - an unbacked license claim. (Flag; the fix is execution, decide in planning.)
- Layout claims: root README and plugin/src/lume/README describe the plugin/ layout correctly post-0008; verify no residual .lume/engine or '227 tests' references survive (none found in a grep, but confirm during execution).

sections.heading: 3. Link graph: current vs desired
sections.body: CURRENT: docs are orphans - no markdown/wiki links between README, docs/*.md, or the plugin READMEs. Opening the repo in Obsidian yields disconnected notes. DESIRED (strand 3): one connected graph rooted at README.md. Minimum edges: README -> install (self or plugin/README), README -> docs/vision, scope, constraints, questions, README -> plugin/src/lume/README (internals) and SKILL. docs/ cross-link where they reference each other (vision<->scope<->constraints<->questions already cross-reference in prose: e.g. constraints/questions mention each other - convert those prose mentions to links). Decide link style (F3): Obsidian [[wikilinks]] (vault-native, but render oddly on GitHub) vs relative markdown links (render on both GitHub and Obsidian). Lean: relative markdown links - they work in Obsidian AND on GitHub, where adopters first meet the repo.

sections.heading: 4. Install-for-users gap
sections.body: TODAY: plugin/README.md has the install path (/plugin marketplace add neilgfoster/lume; /plugin install lume@lume), python3 requirement, first command (lume seed), and the loop. Root README has a short install pointer to plugin/README. GAP: install lives in two places (root pointer + plugin/README) at risk of drift; no single canonical 'Install' that a user lands on first; the live-session caveat (install proven only mechanically) is not stated where a user decides to install. Decide (F4): canonical install location and how the other doc references rather than duplicates it. Lean: canonical install in the root README (the vault root + GitHub face + adversarial front door), plugin/README links to it for the in-plugin reader.

sections.heading: 5. Adversarial README material (why NOT to use lume) + evidence status
sections.body:
Honest disqualifiers/limitations the README must lead with, each tagged [E]=evidenced or [U]=not-yet-evidenced:
- [U] Built by and for ONE person; zero external users. n>1 is possible but unproven (0008 retro: install proven mechanically via a subprocess smoke test, NOT in a live Claude Code session).
- [U] The central value claim - that the ceremony (DoD, gates, JSON state) buys back more time than it costs - is UNMEASURED (questions.md assumption #4 still open; no measurement workstream exists). The framework currently grades its own homework: every iteration was reviewed by its own author, a sample of one.
- [E] Synchronous only - no unattended/background execution (constraints.md; vision non-goals).
- [E] Solo-operator - no multi-operator identity, locking, or handover (scope.md out-of-scope; not built).
- [E] Deliberately scaffolding meant to SHRINK as Claude Code grows; may be superseded by native features (constraints.md 'stay true to intent').
- [U] If you do not work in short, interrupted, multi-session bursts across parallel workstreams, the overhead likely does not pay off (vision.md primary-user JTBD - it is built for that operator, not the general case).
- [E] Early: v0.1.0; the only validation is self-dogfooding.
EVIDENCE-BACKED claims lume MAY fairly make: [E] 313 passing tests; [E] deterministic, stdlib-only, zero runtime deps; [E] installs as a plugin and bootstraps a fresh repo (smoke-tested - but mechanically, mark it); [E] used to build itself across 8 closed workstreams (visible in .lume/workstreams). RULE for execution: no claim without an [E] citation or a [U] flag in the prose. NOTE: ADOPTERS.md (section 1 + fork F8) operationalises the honesty policy - the 'zero external users' [U] claim becomes literally checkable by reading the file; as it gains entries, that disqualifier weakens on evidence, not assertion.

sections.heading: 6. Scope held / deferred
sections.body: No doc rewrites this iteration; artifact is discovery.json only. No engine code. Out: relitigating settled design decisions, marketing polish, docs for non-existent features. Deferred to PLANNING: resolve forks F1-F6 and slice execution (likely: E1 reconcile docs/ + banners; E2 the adversarial+install root README; E3 vault link graph + LICENSE fix; closeout). The honesty rule (evidence-or-flag) and the 'preserve historical, do not silently rewrite' rule are the load-bearing constraints carried into execution.

sections.heading: 7. Open forks for planning
sections.body:
F1 per-doc disposition: confirm keep/update/historical for each (section 1) and the banner wording convention for historical docs. Lean: a short '> Status: historical design record (2026-06-09). For current state see [[README]].' banner.
F2 which README is the adversary: repo-root README (vault root + GitHub face) carries the adversarial/humble/why-not + evidence rule; plugin/README stays install-focused and links to it. Lean: yes, root is the adversary; align plugin/README tone but keep it lean.
F3 link style: relative markdown links (work on GitHub AND Obsidian) vs [[wikilinks]]. Lean: relative markdown links; optionally commit a minimal .obsidian/ later (decide F6).
F4 install canonical location + anti-duplication. Lean: canonical in root README, plugin/README links to it.
F5 LICENSE: add an MIT LICENSE file to match plugin.json, or change the manifest. Lean: add MIT LICENSE (the manifest already asserts it).
F6 vault config: commit an .obsidian/ folder (opinionated, may churn) vs rely on convention (any folder is a vault). Lean: rely on convention, no committed .obsidian/ unless a specific setting is needed.
F7 evidence mechanism: inline parenthetical citations ('(313 tests)', '(0008 retro)') vs a dedicated Evidence/Status section. Lean: inline tags in prose + a short 'Status & evidence' section at the end of the README for the load-bearing claims.
F8 ADOPTERS.md shape: the entry format and how machine-readable to make it now. Options: a markdown table (project | adopter | link | since), a bullet list, or a fenced structured block (YAML/JSON-ish) per adopter. Lean: a markdown table with a fixed column set - human-readable on GitHub/Obsidian AND trivially parseable later; seed it with the single honest row for lume itself, and document the one-line 'how to add yourself (open a PR)' process. Defer any actual lume verb that reads it (that is a later workstream, per the objective's 'eventually').
F9 phasing of execution slices (now includes creating ADOPTERS.md and linking it into the graph).
